annotations.label: Noetherian ring

anchor.snippet: is a ring in which every ideal is finitely generated

annotations.note: `IsNoetherianRing R` is defined via `IsNoetherian R R`, i.e. every ideal is finitely generated.

annotations.label: Hilbert's basis theorem

anchor.snippet: The fact that polynomial rings over a field are Noetherian is called Hilbert's basis theorem

annotations.note: Mathlib proves Hilbert's basis theorem in the stronger form: any polynomial ring over a Noetherian ring is Noetherian.

annotations.label: Noetherian property preserved by constructions

anchor.snippet: if a commutative ring R is Noetherian, the same is true for every polynomial ring over it

annotations.note: Stability under polynomial extensions is `Polynomial.isNoetherianRing`; quotients and finite products also carry `IsNoetherianRing` instances.

annotations.label: Noetherian local rings have finite Krull dimension

anchor.snippet: if a ring is Noetherian, then it satisfies the descending chain condition on prime ideals

annotations.note: Krull's height theorem is formalized and bounds heights by generator count, but I did not find an instance directly deriving `FiniteRingKrullDim` from `IsLocalRing` + `IsNoetherianRing`.

annotations.label: Primary ideal

anchor.snippet: is said to be primary if Q is proper and whenever

annotations.note: `Ideal.IsPrimary I` is the standard definition: `I ≠ ⊤` and `xy ∈ I → x ∈ I ∨ y ∈ I.radical`.

annotations.label: Primary ideals in Z

anchor.snippet: In Z , the primary ideals are precisely the ideals of the form

annotations.note: I found no Mathlib lemma classifying primary ideals of ℤ as `(0)` or `(p^n)`.

annotations.label: Lasker–Noether theorem

anchor.value: Lasker-Noether Theorem — Let R be a comm

annotations.note: `Submodule.isLasker` shows every submodule of a Noetherian module admits a primary decomposition, generalizing the classical ring statement.

annotations.label: Radicals of primary decomposition form the assassinator

anchor.snippet: the set of all radicals, that is, the set

annotations.note: Lasker.lean shows the radicals of a minimal primary decomposition equal `N.associatedPrimes` (the assassinator).

annotations.label: Localization of a ring

anchor.snippet: The localization is a formal way to introduce the "denominators" to a given ring or a module

annotations.note: `IsLocalization M S` is the predicate characterising the localization of `R` at a submonoid `M`; the concrete construction is `Localization M`.

annotations.label: Rationals as localization of integers

anchor.snippet: The archetypal example is the construction of the ring Q of rational numbers from the ring Z of integers

annotations.note: `Rat.isFractionRing` is an instance witnessing that ℚ is the localization of ℤ at the nonzero divisors.

annotations.label: Completion

anchor.snippet: A completion is any of several related functors on rings and modules that result in complete topological rings and modules

annotations.note: `AdicCompletion I M` formalizes the I-adic completion of a module; the general topological completion is `UniformSpace.Completion`.

annotations.label: Zariski topology on the spectrum

anchor.snippet: The Zariski topology defines a topology on the spectrum of a ring

annotations.note: `PrimeSpectrum.zariskiTopology` is the topology on `PrimeSpectrum R` whose closed sets are zero loci of subsets of `R`.
